title: Pleurothallis antioquiensis
intro: Pleurothallis antioquiensis, also known as the Antioquia Pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis antioquiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.

The species Pleurothallis antioquiensis is found in the Andean region of South America, particularly in Colombia.
